text: The only real stumbling block is fear of failure. In cooking you've got to have a what-the-hell attitude.
author: Julia Child

text: A recipe has no soul. You, as the cook, must bring soul to the recipe.
author: Thomas Keller

text: Good food is the foundation of genuine happiness.
author: Auguste Escoffier

text: Cooking is like painting or writing a song. Just as there are only so many notes or colors, there are only so many flavors.
author: Gordon Ramsay

text: Your body is not a temple, it's an amusement park. Enjoy the ride.
author: Anthony Bourdain

text: To me, there's no great chef without a great team.
author: Daniel Boulud

text: The most important class you can take is technique. A great chef is first a great technician.
author: Jacques Pépin

text: When you run a kitchen, no matter how crazy and chaotic it gets, you have to be the calm one.
author: Nina Compton

text: Cooking will never be good if it isn't made with love for the person it's being made for.
author: Paul Bocuse

text: When you acknowledge there is no such thing as perfect food, only the idea of it, then the real purpose of striving toward perfection becomes clear: to make people happy.
author: Thomas Keller

text: Chefs don't make mistakes; they make new dishes.
author: Elizabeth Brigg

text: Good food is a right, not a privilege.
author: Alice Waters

text: No one is born a great cook, one learns by doing.
author: Julia Child

text: Cooking is multisensory. It's made for the eyes, the mouth, the nose, the ear, and the soul.
author: Pierre Gagnaire

text: If you're a happy person around food, you can be a professional chef. It's fuelled by passion.
author: Ranveer Brar

text: Food is as much about the moment, the occasion, the location and the company as it is about the taste.
author: Heston Blumenthal

text: If you want to become a great chef, you have to work with great chefs.
author: Gordon Ramsay

text: I'm not a chef. I'm a passionate home cook who loves to feed people.
author: Maya Angelou

text: Pull up a chair. Take a taste. Come join us. Life is so endlessly delicious.
author: Ruth Reichl

text: In the hands of an able cook, fish can become an inexhaustible source of perpetual delight.
author: Jean Anthelme Brillat-Savarin

text: Cooking is at once child's play and adult joy. And cooking done with care is an act of love.
author: Craig Claiborne

text: People who love to eat are always the best people.
author: Julia Child

text: One cannot think well, love well, sleep well, if one has not dined well.
author: Virginia Woolf

text: The kitchen is a place of adventure and disastrous experiments as much as nourishing creations.
author: Yotam Ottolenghi

text: Life is uncertain. Eat dessert first.
author: Ernestine Ulmer

text: Cooking is the ultimate giving.
author: Jamie Oliver

text: The secret ingredient is always love.
author: Buddy Valastro

text: First we eat, then we do everything else.
author: M.F.K. Fisher

text: Laughter is brightest in the place where the food is.
author: Irish Proverb

text: Food is our common ground, a universal experience.
author: James Beard

text: The only time to eat diet food is while waiting for the steak to cook.
author: Julia Child

text: There is no sincerer love than the love of food.
author: George Bernard Shaw

text: In cooking, as in all the arts, simplicity is the sign of perfection.
author: Curnonsky

text: Tell me what you eat, and I will tell you what you are.
author: Jean Anthelme Brillat-Savarin

text: Cooking is an observation-based process that you can't do if you're too busy following a recipe.
author: Alton Brown

text: Food, to me, is always about cooking and eating with those you love and care for.
author: David Chang

text: The measure of achievement is not winning awards. It's doing something that you appreciate.
author: Arundhati Roy

text: A good cook is like a sorceress who dispenses happiness.
author: Elsa Schiaparelli

text: I cook with wine. Sometimes I even add it to the food.
author: W.C. Fields

text: Cooking and eating food is a way to show love and care.
author: Lidia Bastianich

text: The kitchen is where we deal with the elements of the universe. It is where we come to understand our past and ourselves.
author: Laura Esquivel

text: The way you make an omelet reveals your character.
author: Anthony Bourdain

text: Salt is born of the purest parents: the sun and the sea.
author: Pythagoras

text: Flavor is the main thing in cooking. We have the ingredients — we just have to bring out the best in them.
author: Massimo Bottura

text: If more of us valued food and cheer and song above hoarded gold, it would be a merrier world.
author: J.R.R. Tolkien

text: Rice is born in water and must die in wine.
author: Italian Proverb

text: To eat is a necessity, but to eat intelligently is an art.
author: François de La Rochefoucauld

text: My weaknesses have always been food and men — in that order.
author: Dolly Parton

text: Eating is so intimate. It's very sensual. When you invite someone to sit at your table and you want to cook for them, you're inviting a person into your life.
author: Maya Angelou

text: Cooking is like snow — if you don't deal with it right away, it becomes a problem.
author: Nora Ephron
